title: Reactome (CC0) pathway + gene/protein-participation ETL
context: Reactome is a CC0-licensed open pathway database providing gene/protein-participation-in-pathway associations. Once the identifier-normalization service (oncogene-kg-id-002) and license analysis (oncogene-kg-license-002) are in place, Reactome pathway data can be integrated to extend the knowledge graph with pathway context for genes and variants. This is a lower-risk extraction because Reactome's CC0 license is unambiguous.
objective: Implement a Reactome-to-Biolink ETL pipeline that ingests a pinned Reactome release, extracts gene/protein-participation-in-pathway associations, normalizes identifiers via the normalization service, attaches complete provenance, and passes all CI gates.
acceptanceCriteria: Transforms a pinned, checksummed Reactome release to Biolink pathway participation associations.
Gene/protein entities are normalized to HGNC/Ensembl and pathway entities to Reactome CURIEs via the normalization service.
Every edge carries complete provenance: source=Reactome, record ID, license=CC0, version, method, pipeline commit.
Output passes Biolink/SHACL validation and all CI gates.
Reactome's allowlist entry is marked approved before ingestion begins.
ETL is re-runnable and produces a stable output for a given pinned input version.
output: etl/reactome/ directory containing the Reactome ETL pipeline code, committed via PR with CI green.
